title: Stripe Payments Infrastructure Explained
subtitle: How Stripe actually works — payments, subscriptions, webhooks, and the integration patterns that hold up in production
description: A practical course on Stripe built from their developer docs and engineering posts. Covers the payment model (PaymentIntents, customers, products, prices), subscriptions and billing, webhooks done right, dispute handling, tax and compliance basics, and the integration patterns that survive contact with real customers and real money.
audience: Developers and technical founders integrating Stripe into a product. Comfortable with REST APIs and HTTP; some familiarity with payments helps but isn't required.
prerequisites: Comfortable with REST APIs and HTTP
Have a Stripe account (test mode is free)
Familiarity with at least one server-side language
outcomes: Understand the Stripe data model — Customers, PaymentIntents, Products, Prices, Subscriptions
Accept a payment end-to-end using Stripe.js + Checkout or Elements
Build subscription billing including trials, upgrades, and dunning
Handle webhooks correctly — verification, idempotency, retries
Manage disputes, refunds, and customer support cases
Apply tax and compliance basics (Stripe Tax, SCA, 3DS)
Ship a Stripe integration that survives real customers and edge cases

modules.summary: Stripe's data model — Customers, PaymentIntents, Products, Prices — and how the pieces fit together.

lessons.title: PaymentIntents — the modern payment primitive

lessons.objective: Use idempotency keys to make payment operations safe to retry.
